---
title: "Wader Hotspots Denmark"
author: "Ismael Kreie Engelbreth Larsen"
date: "2026-06-01"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Load required libraries
library(tidyverse)  # data manipulation and ggplot2
library(sf)         # spatial data handling
library(MASS)       # kde2d for kernel density estimation

# Set a consistent theme for plots
theme_set(theme_minimal())
```

```{r read-gbif-csv}
# Load the GBIF occurrence data
# Source: GBIF Occurrence Download, doi:10.15468/dl.tt323j
# Downloaded 01 June 2026, CC BY-NC 4.0
# Key columns used in analysis:
# species - character (scientific name)
# decimalLatitude, decimalLongitude - numeric (WGS84 coordinates)
# month, year - numeric (used for temporal filtering)

df <- suppressWarnings(
  read_tsv("../data/gbif_scolopacidae_dk.csv",
           show_col_types = FALSE)
)

cat("Rows loaded:", nrow(df), "\n")
cat("Columns:", ncol(df), "\n")
```

```{r clean-data}
# Select relevant columns and apply cleaning filters
df_clean <- df %>%
  dplyr::select(
    gbifID,
    species,
    decimalLatitude,
    decimalLongitude,
    month,
    year
  ) %>%
  # Remove records with missing coordinates
  filter(!is.na(decimalLatitude), !is.na(decimalLongitude)) %>%
  # Restrict to Denmark's geographic bounding box
  # Encompasses mainland Denmark and all major islands including Bornholm
  filter(
    decimalLatitude  >= 54.5, decimalLatitude  <= 57.8,
    decimalLongitude >= 8.0,  decimalLongitude <= 15.3
  ) %>%
  # Restrict to study period
  filter(year >= 2016, year <= 2025) %>%
  # Filter to peak migration months only
  # Spring passage: April-May, Autumn passage: July-September
  # Scolopacidae are predominantly migratory in Denmark;
  # restricting to these months focuses the analysis on passage birds
  # (Meltofte et al., 2019)
  filter(month %in% c(4, 5, 7, 8, 9))

cat("Rows after cleaning:", nrow(df_clean), "\n")

# Species frequency overview
df_clean %>%
  count(species, sort = TRUE) %>%
  head(15) %>%
  print()
```

```{r crs-transformation}
# Convert cleaned data frame to spatial object (sf)
# Input CRS: EPSG 4326 (WGS84 geographic coordinates, as provided by GBIF)
df_sf <- df_clean %>%
  st_as_sf(coords = c("decimalLongitude", "decimalLatitude"), crs = 4326)

# Reproject to EPSG 25832 (UTM zone 32N)
# Standard projected CRS for Denmark; uses metres for accurate distance calculations
df_sf <- st_transform(df_sf, crs = 25832)

cat("Spatial object created with", nrow(df_sf), "points\n")
```

```{r raw-observation-map}
# Raw observation dot map, 10% random sample for rendering speed
# The full dataset of 735,555 points is downsampled to 10% for rendering;
# the spatial distribution is preserved.
set.seed(42)  # ensures reproducibility of the random sample
df_sample <- df_sf %>% slice_sample(prop = 0.1)

ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = df_sample, alpha = 0.1, size = 0.3, colour = "steelblue") +
  labs(
    title = "Scolopacidae observations in Denmark (2016-2025)",
    subtitle = "10% random sample - peak migration months (Apr-May, Jul-Sep)",
    caption = "Source: GBIF / DOFbasen, BirdLife Denmark (doi:10.15468/dl.tt323j)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

ggsave("../output/raw_observations.png",
       width = 8, height = 7, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
```

```{r kde-compute}
# Kernel Density Estimation (KDE) of wader observation density
# Method: kde2d from the MASS package (Venables & Ripley, 2002)
# following Silverman (1986)

# Sample 200,000 points from 735,555 for computational efficiency
# This represents 27% of the cleaned dataset
set.seed(42)  # ensures reproducibility of the random sample
coords <- st_coordinates(df_sf)
coords <- coords[sample(nrow(coords), 200000), ]

# Compute KDE on a 300x300 grid across the full data extent
kde <- kde2d(
  x = coords[, 1],
  y = coords[, 2],
  n = 300,
  lims = c(range(coords[, 1]), range(coords[, 2]))
)

# Convert KDE output to a data frame and rescale density values to 0-1
# Cap at 99th percentile to prevent a single intensively monitored site
# from dominating the colour scale
kde_df <- expand.grid(x = kde$x, y = kde$y) %>%
  mutate(
    density = as.vector(kde$z),
    density_scaled = (density - min(density)) /
                     (max(density) - min(density)),
    density_capped = pmin(density_scaled, quantile(density_scaled, 0.99)),
    density_capped = (density_capped - min(density_capped)) /
                     (max(density_capped) - min(density_capped))
  )

cat("KDE calculated and rescaled -", nrow(kde_df), "grid cells\n")
```

```{r kde-map}
# Load high-resolution Denmark boundary from DAWA (Danmarks Adressers Web API)
# Official Danish geodata source
denmark_outline <- st_read(
  "https://api.dataforsyningen.dk/landsdele?format=geojson",
  quiet = TRUE
) %>%
  st_union() %>%           # merge all regions into single national outline
  st_transform(crs = 25832)

# Filter to top 40% of density values for display
# Low-density cells are excluded so the map shows distinct hotspots
# rather than a uniform wash across the whole country
kde_plot <- kde_df %>%
  filter(density_capped > quantile(density_capped, 0.6))

# Calculate min and max of filtered data
# so legend labels match the actual plotted range
kde_min <- min(kde_plot$density_capped)
kde_max <- max(kde_plot$density_capped)

ggplot() +
  # White land background which distinguishes land from sea colour
  geom_sf(data = denmark_outline, fill = "white", colour = NA) +
  # KDE density surface
  geom_tile(data = kde_plot,
            aes(x = x, y = y, fill = density_capped)) +
  # Country border on top for geographic reference
  geom_sf(data = denmark_outline, fill = NA,
          colour = "grey20", linewidth = 0.5) +
  # Sequential white-orange-red palette
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colours = c("#fff7ec", "#fee8c8", "#fdd49e",
                "#fdbb84", "#fc8d59", "#e34a33", "#b30000"),
    name = "Observation\ndensity",
    limits = c(kde_min, kde_max),
    breaks = c(kde_min, (kde_min + kde_max) / 2, kde_max),
    labels = c("Low", "Medium", "High")
  ) +
  # Bounding box in UTM metres, shows all of Denmark without clipping
  coord_sf(crs = 25832, expand = TRUE,
           xlim = c(430000, 920000),
           ylim = c(6030000, 6420000)) +
  labs(
    title = "Observation hotspots of migratory waders in Denmark",
    subtitle = "Kernel Density Estimation of Scolopacidae records, peak migration 2016-2025",
    caption = "Data: GBIF / DOFbasen, BirdLife Denmark (doi:10.15468/dl.tt323j). Boundary: DAWA/Dataforsyningen."
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#eef4f8", colour = NA),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold",
                              colour = "grey10", margin = margin(b = 4)),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40",
                                 margin = margin(b = 8)),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 7, colour = "grey50",
                                margin = margin(t = 6)),
    legend.position = c(0.92, 0.75),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 8, colour = "grey20"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 7, colour = "grey30"),
    plot.margin = margin(12, 20, 8, 12)
  )

ggsave("../output/kde_hotspots.png",
       width = 8, height = 7, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
```

```{r load-miljøportal-data}
# Load Danish EU Bird Protection Areas (EF-fuglebeskyttelsesområder)
# Source: Danmarks Miljøportal / Arealdata API
# https://arealdata.miljoeportal.dk/datasets/urn:dmp:ds:natura2000-fuglebeskyttelse
# Designated under the EU Birds Directive (2009/147/EC)
# Last updated: 1 June 2026

# The file is included in the data/ folder of this repository.
# The download logic below is retained for reproducibility -
# if the file is deleted or the code is run in a new environment,
# it will be re-downloaded automatically from Danmarks Miljøportal.
if (!file.exists("../data/bird_protection_areas.gpkg")) {

  temp_zip <- tempfile(fileext = ".zip")
  download.file(
    "https://arealdata-api.miljoeportal.dk/download/daie/FUGLE_BES_OMR_SHAPE.zip",
    destfile = temp_zip,
    mode = "wb"
  )

  temp_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "zipfolder")
  unzip(temp_zip, exdir = temp_dir)

  shp_path <- list.files(temp_dir, pattern = "\\.shp$",
                         recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)[1]

  bird_protection <- st_read(shp_path, quiet = TRUE) %>%
    st_transform(crs = 25832)

  st_write(bird_protection, "../data/bird_protection_areas.gpkg",
           delete_dsn = TRUE, quiet = TRUE)

  cat("Downloaded and saved to data folder\n")

} else {

  bird_protection <- st_read("../data/bird_protection_areas.gpkg", quiet = TRUE)
  cat("Loaded from data folder\n")

}

cat("Bird protection areas loaded:", nrow(bird_protection), "\n")
```

```{r kde-hotspot-protection-join}
# Spatial join: identify which top KDE hotspot cells fall within protection areas
# The 500 highest-density KDE grid cells are used as point representatives
# of the core hotspot areas

top_hotspots <- kde_df %>%
  arrange(desc(density_capped)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 500) %>%
  st_as_sf(coords = c("x", "y"), crs = 25832)

# Point-within-polygon join using st_within (sf package)
# Each hotspot cell is matched to the protection area it falls inside, if any
hotspot_join <- st_join(top_hotspots, bird_protection, join = st_within)

# Summarise results
n_protected <- sum(!is.na(hotspot_join$Objektnavn))
n_total     <- nrow(hotspot_join)

cat("Top KDE hotspot cells within protection areas:",
    n_protected, "out of", n_total, "\n")
cat("Percentage:", round(n_protected / n_total * 100), "%\n")

# Which protection areas contain the most hotspot cells?
hotspot_join %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  filter(!is.na(Objektnavn)) %>%
  count(Objektnavn, sort = TRUE) %>%
  print()
```

```{r kde-overlay-map}
# Overlay map: KDE hotspot surface with Natura 2000 protection areas
# Transparent green fill shows protection areas without obscuring the density surface

kde_plot <- kde_df %>%
  filter(density_capped > quantile(density_capped, 0.6))

kde_min <- min(kde_plot$density_capped)
kde_max <- max(kde_plot$density_capped)

ggplot() +
  # White land background
  geom_sf(data = denmark_outline, fill = "white", colour = NA) +
  # KDE density surface
  geom_tile(data = kde_plot,
            aes(x = x, y = y, fill = density_capped)) +
  # Natura 2000 Bird Protection Areas with transparent green overlay
  geom_sf(data = bird_protection,
          fill = "#52b788", colour = "#2d6a4f", alpha = 0.4, linewidth = 0.3) +
  # Country border on top
  geom_sf(data = denmark_outline, fill = NA,
          colour = "grey20", linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colours = c("#fff7ec", "#fee8c8", "#fdd49e",
                "#fdbb84", "#fc8d59", "#e34a33", "#b30000"),
    name = "Observation\ndensity",
    limits = c(kde_min, kde_max),
    breaks = c(kde_min, (kde_min + kde_max) / 2, kde_max),
    labels = c("Low", "Medium", "High")
  ) +
  coord_sf(crs = 25832, expand = TRUE,
           xlim = c(430000, 920000),
           ylim = c(6030000, 6420000)) +
  # Manual legend key for protection areas
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = 730000, xmax = 750000,
           ymin = 6045000, ymax = 6055000,
           fill = "#52b788", colour = "#2d6a4f", alpha = 0.4, linewidth = 0.3) +
  annotate("text", x = 755000, y = 6050000,
           label = "Bird Protection Area (Natura 2000)",
           size = 2.5, hjust = 0, colour = "grey20") +
  labs(
    title = "Wader observation hotspots and EU Bird Protection Areas",
    subtitle = "KDE of Scolopacidae peak migration records (2016-2025) with Natura 2000 SPAs overlaid (green)",
    caption = "Data: GBIF / DOFbasen, BirdLife Denmark (doi:10.15468/dl.tt323j). Protected areas: Danmarks Miljøportal. Boundary: DAWA."
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#eef4f8", colour = NA),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold",
                              colour = "grey10", margin = margin(b = 4)),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40",
                                 margin = margin(b = 8)),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 7, colour = "grey50",
                                margin = margin(t = 6)),
    legend.position = c(0.92, 0.75),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 8, colour = "grey20"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 7, colour = "grey30"),
    plot.margin = margin(12, 20, 8, 12)
  )

ggsave("../output/kde_with_protection.png",
       width = 8, height = 7, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
```

```{r session-info}
sink("../session_info.txt")
sessionInfo()
sink()
```
